title: Why some friendships survive long silences
summary: Argue what allows certain friendships to pick up easily after months or years without contact.
topic: Why some friendships survive long silences
prompt: Write 3 paragraphs (350–500 words) arguing why a few friendships can resume after a long silence while others can't. Be concrete: use a specific friend and a specific reunion. Use relative clauses, modals, and at least one linker of contrast.
hintStarters: There is a friend I haven't seen since…

hintVocab.phrase: to pick up where we left off
hintVocab.meaningVi: tiếp tục từ chỗ đã dừng
tha thứ cho sự chậm trễ
tình bạn ít cần chăm sóc

sampleText:
There is a friend I haven't seen since a winter in Krakow eight years ago, and yet, when she finally called last month, we picked up where we left off in less than a minute. We didn't apologise. We didn't summarise our lives. She asked me a question I would never have answered for a stranger, and I gave her the long, messy answer it deserved. I have been thinking ever since about why some friendships survive these long silences and others quietly close.

What survives, I'd argue, is a shared way of paying attention. The friends who come back easily are the ones who remember the small, unflattering details — the song you hated, the boss who never said your name correctly, the dream you almost cancelled. They don't keep these things to be sentimental; they keep them because they were paying attention in the first place. If you both still notice the world in the same way, you don't need to message every week. Whereas a friend who only liked the holiday version of you will struggle to find you again in everyday life, a friend who liked the tired Tuesday version will recognise you anywhere. That is why some reunions feel like warm rain, and others feel like a polite interview.

Some people will say that any friendship which goes quiet for years is over, and that we should let it rest. I might be wrong, but I disagree. A friendship doesn't end the way a job ends, on a specific Friday. It thins, and then sometimes thickens again, like soup. If we treated every long silence as proof that the bond was gone, we would close many doors too quickly. The kinder rule is to assume the door is still there, slightly ajar, until somebody pushes it shut on purpose. Last month's phone call wouldn't have happened under any other rule. My friend had simply been busy raising a daughter and rebuilding a kitchen, and I had been busy in my own way. Neither of us had stopped caring; we had stopped writing. That is a smaller crime than people think, and it is one of the few crimes a good friendship will forgive without asking for an explanation.

sampleAnnotations.phrase: picked up where we left off
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: tiếp tục từ chỗ đã dừng
câu trả lời dài và lộn xộn
đã suy nghĩ từ đó đến giờ
những chi tiết nhỏ, không tâng bốc
phiên bản kỳ nghỉ của bạn
phiên bản mệt mỏi của thứ Ba
một cuộc phỏng vấn lịch sự
cách một công việc kết thúc

sampleAnnotations.phrase: thins, and then sometimes thickens again
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: mỏng đi, rồi đôi khi dày trở lại

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Present perfect with 'since'
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'There is a friend I haven't seen since a winter in Krakow' dùng hiện tại hoàn thành với 'since' để gắn một tình huống hiện tại với một mốc thời gian trong quá khứ.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'There is a friend I haven't seen since a winter in Krakow' uses the present perfect with 'since' to anchor a present situation to a past point in time.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Linker 'whereas' for direct contrast
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'Whereas a friend who only liked the holiday version of you will struggle…, a friend who liked the tired Tuesday version will recognise you anywhere' dùng 'whereas' để so sánh hai trường hợp song song.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'Whereas a friend who only liked the holiday version of you will struggle…, a friend who liked the tired Tuesday version will recognise you anywhere' uses 'whereas' to contrast two parallel cases directly.

sampleTranslationVi:
Có một người bạn tôi chưa gặp từ một mùa đông ở Kraków tám năm trước, vậy mà khi cuối cùng cô ấy gọi tháng trước, chúng tôi tiếp tục từ chỗ đã dừng chưa đầy một phút. Chúng tôi không xin lỗi. Chúng tôi không tóm tắt cuộc sống của nhau. Cô ấy hỏi tôi một câu hỏi mà tôi sẽ không bao giờ trả lời cho một người lạ, và tôi đưa ra câu trả lời dài, lộn xộn mà nó xứng đáng. Tôi đã suy nghĩ từ đó về việc tại sao một số tình bạn sống sót qua những khoảng lặng dài này và một số khác lặng lẽ khép lại.

Điều sống sót, tôi cho rằng, là một cách chú ý chung. Những người bạn quay lại dễ dàng là những người nhớ những chi tiết nhỏ, không tâng bốc — bài hát bạn ghét, ông sếp chưa bao giờ gọi đúng tên bạn, giấc mơ mà bạn suýt hủy. Họ không giữ những điều này để ủy mị; họ giữ vì ngay từ đầu họ đã chú ý. Nếu cả hai vẫn cảm nhận thế giới theo cùng một cách, bạn không cần nhắn tin mỗi tuần. Trong khi một người bạn chỉ thích phiên bản kỳ nghỉ của bạn sẽ chật vật tìm bạn trong cuộc sống thường ngày, một người bạn thích phiên bản mệt mỏi của thứ Ba sẽ nhận ra bạn ở bất cứ đâu. Đó là lý do tại sao một số cuộc đoàn tụ cảm thấy như cơn mưa ấm, và những cuộc khác cảm thấy như một cuộc phỏng vấn lịch sự.

Một số người sẽ nói rằng bất kỳ tình bạn nào lặng im trong nhiều năm là đã kết thúc, và chúng ta nên để nó yên. Có thể tôi sai, nhưng tôi không đồng ý. Một tình bạn không kết thúc theo cách một công việc kết thúc, vào một ngày thứ Sáu cụ thể. Nó mỏng đi, rồi đôi khi dày trở lại, như súp. Nếu chúng ta coi mỗi khoảng lặng dài là bằng chứng rằng tình cảm đã mất, chúng ta sẽ đóng quá nhiều cửa quá nhanh. Quy tắc tử tế hơn là giả định rằng cánh cửa vẫn còn đó, khép hờ, cho đến khi có ai đó cố tình đẩy nó đóng lại. Cuộc gọi tháng trước đã không xảy ra dưới bất kỳ quy tắc nào khác. Bạn tôi chỉ đơn giản là bận nuôi một cô con gái và xây lại bếp, và tôi cũng bận theo cách của mình. Không ai trong chúng tôi ngừng quan tâm; chúng tôi đã ngừng viết. Đó là một tội lỗi nhỏ hơn người ta nghĩ, và là một trong số ít tội lỗi mà một tình bạn tốt sẽ tha thứ mà không cần lời giải thích.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses the present perfect with 'since' correctly?
options: I don't seen her since a winter in Krakow eight years ago.
There is a friend I haven't seen since a winter in Krakow eight years ago.
There is a friend I am not seen since a winter in Krakow eight years ago.
I haven't see her since a winter in Krakow eight years ago.
mcQuestions.explanation: Present perfect = 'have/has + past participle' (seen), with 'since' to mark the starting point.
mcQuestions.hint: 'Haven't' + past participle + 'since' + past point in time.

options: Whereas of a friend who only liked the holiday version of you, will struggle.
Whereas a friend who only liked the holiday version of you will struggle, a friend who liked the tired Tuesday version will recognise you anywhere.
A friend who only liked the holiday version, whereas, will struggle to find you.
Whereas, a friend who only liked the holiday version, but recognise you anywhere.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Whereas' joins two parallel clauses to highlight contrast; both halves should be full clauses.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the most natural conditional sentence.
options: If we treated every long silence as proof, we would close many doors too quickly.
If we treat every long silence as proof, we would close many doors.
If we treated every long silence as proof, we will close many doors.
If we will treat every long silence as proof, we would close doors.
mcQuestions.explanation: Second conditional = past simple after 'if' + 'would' + base verb in the main clause.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses a relative clause correctly?
options: Any friendship which goes quiet for years is over, some people will say.
Any friendship that goes quiet, for years is over.
Any friendship what goes quiet for years is over.
Any friendship who goes quiet for years is over.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Which' is correct for things (here, a friendship treated as a thing); 'who' is for people, and 'what' is wrong as a relative pronoun.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses 'might' for a hedged opinion?
options: I might be wrong, but I disagree.
I might wrong, but I disagree.
I am might wrong, but I disagree.
I might to be wrong, but I disagree.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Might + bare infinitive' is the correct modal structure; 'to be' is wrong after modals.

options: My friend simply had been busy raising a daughter.
My friend had been simply busy raising a daughter.
My friend had simply been busy raising a daughter and rebuilding a kitchen.
Simply my friend had been busy raising a daughter.
mcQuestions.explanation: Adverbs of degree like 'simply' usually go between the auxiliary and the participle: 'had simply been'.
mcQuestions.hint: Between the first auxiliary and the next verb.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence has the correct tense for an ongoing thought?
options: I have been thinking ever since about why some friendships survive.
I am thinking ever since about why some friendships survive.
I thought ever since about why some friendships survive.
I have think ever since about why some friendships survive.
mcQuestions.explanation: Present perfect continuous ('have been + verb-ing') describes an ongoing mental activity starting in the past.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses the modal 'would' correctly in a hypothetical comment?
options: Last month's phone call wouldn't have happened under any other rule.
Last month's phone call wouldn't happens under any other rule.
Last month's phone call wouldn't to happen under any other rule.
Last month's phone call would don't happen under any other rule.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Wouldn't have happened' = third conditional / past hypothetical; modal + 'have' + past participle.
mcQuestions.hint: Modal + 'have' + past participle for past hypothetical.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the most natural register for a reflective B1 essay.
options: That is why some reunions feel like warm rain, and others feel like a polite interview.
That is why some reunions are warm rain feels, and others polite interview.
That is why some reunion feels warm rain, and others feel polite interview.
That is why some reunions like warm rain and others like a polite interview is.
mcQuestions.explanation: Use 'feel like + noun' with subject–verb agreement and a comma before the contrasting clause.
mcQuestions.hint: Subject + 'feel like' + noun; clauses joined by 'and'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses the correct linker for additional contrast?
options: It thins, and then sometimes thickens again, like soup.
It thins, however, then sometimes thickens again, like soup.
It thins, despite, sometimes thickens again, like soup.
It thins, although it thickens again sometimes.
mcQuestions.explanation: Simple coordination with 'and then' works best here; the other linkers misuse contrast in this position.
mcQuestions.hint: Coordination, not contrast, fits this sequence.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Is there a friend whose silence you've misread? What might be a kinder explanation for the gap than the one you currently believe?
